{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-42271", "cvss": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:H/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-77", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-78", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')"}], "description": "### Impact

Two endpoints used to preview an MCP server before saving it — `POST /mcp-rest/test/connection` and `POST /mcp-rest/test/tools/list` — accepted a full server configuration in the request body, including the `command`, `args`, and `env` fields used by the stdio transport. When called with a stdio configuration, the endpoints attempted to connect, which spawned the supplied command as a subprocess on the proxy host with the privileges of the proxy process.

The endpoints were gated only by a valid proxy API key, with no role check. Any authenticated user — including holders of low-privilege internal-user keys — could therefore run arbitrary commands on the host.

### Patches

Fixed in **`1.83.7`**. Both test endpoints now require the `PROXY_ADMIN` role, bringing them into line with the save endpoint.

### Workarounds

If upgrading is not immediately possible, developers should block `POST /mcp-rest/test/connection` and `POST /mcp-rest/test/tools/list` at their reverse proxy or API gateway.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.80188, "percentile": 0.99568}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-25T23:27:54Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-42271"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-08T04:16:21Z", "published_at": "2026-04-25T23:27:54Z", "references": ["https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/security/advisories/GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g", "https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/releases/tag/v1.83.7-stable", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42271", "https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog?field_cve=CVE-2026-42271", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/BerriAI/litellm/security-advisories/GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm", "summary": "LiteLLM: Authenticated command execution via MCP stdio test endpoints", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T13:07:08Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-v4p8-mg3p-g94g", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.83.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "litellm"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 1.74.2, < 1.83.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: LiteLLM: Authenticated command execution via MCP stdio test endpoints
